description_long: This triumphal arch is a grand, rectangular stone structure featuring three prominent archways at its base, with the central arch being the largest. The facade is richly adorned with detailed sculptural reliefs and decorative panels, especially above the arches and along the upper sections. The structure is divided into multiple horizontal tiers, each separated by projecting cornices and bands of ornamentation. Flanking the arches are pilasters that add vertical emphasis and classical architectural detail. The stonework displays a mix of light tan and gray hues, giving the monument a weathered, historic appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of ancient Roman triumphal arches, exuding a sense of monumentality and celebration.
description: A monumental stone triumphal arch with three archways, ornate reliefs, and classical pilasters, featuring layered horizontal tiers and a weathered, historic look.
description_view_0: Front view showing three archways, detailed reliefs, and decorative panels above the arches.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the structure and the layered horizontal tiers, with minimal decorative detail visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying three archways and ornate reliefs, though some details may differ.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the structure's depth and tiered design with little surface decoration.
